title: Course 2 worked examples + exercises + self-check (lead-time/overdiagnosis demonstrations from aggregate data)
context: The worked examples and exercises for Course 2 (Reading Survival) are a separate authoring task from the core content. Because survival content is patient-facing (HIGH riskTier), all three expert reviewers must sign off: biostatistician, oncologist, and patient advocate. Exercises must demonstrate lead-time bias and overdiagnosis with concrete worked examples from cited aggregate data so that learners can distinguish a real mortality benefit from a survival artifact. The advocate reviewer must confirm plain-language and dignity (no fear-mongering, no false reassurance). No individual prognosis may be implied in any worked example.
objective: Author worked examples, exercises, and self-check items for Course 2: Reading Survival. Core worked examples must demonstrate lead-time bias and overdiagnosis using concrete aggregate data so learners can distinguish a real mortality benefit from a survival artifact. Exercises must let learners practice distinguishing the survival-vs-mortality endpoint distinction. All items must carry the 'Education only — not medical advice' frame. All statistics must be cited with data vintage.
acceptanceCriteria: Includes concrete worked examples demonstrating lead-time bias and overdiagnosis using cited aggregate data, showing how survival can rise with no mortality benefit
Exercises let learners distinguish a real mortality benefit from a survival artifact in at least two different scenarios
Self-check items directly test the 'survival-rise implies screening/treatment benefit' misconception and its correction
Plain-language and dignity reviewed by the patient-advocate reviewer: no fear-mongering, no false reassurance, no implied individual prognosis
All statistical assertions carry register citations with data vintage; citation coverage 100%
Biostatistician/epidemiologist sign-off recorded in the reviewers ledger before merge
Oncologist sign-off recorded in the reviewers ledger confirming clinical accuracy and safe framing before merge
Patient-advocate sign-off recorded in the reviewers ledger confirming plain-language clarity and harm-avoidance before merge
output: Worked examples, exercises, and self-check items for Course 2: Reading Survival — demonstrating lead-time bias and overdiagnosis from aggregate data, with HIGH-tier framing upheld and biostatistician + oncologist + patient-advocate sign-off recorded.
